general-corpus-pandoc-pdf.description: Required General-Corpus CME XML-to-Pandoc workflow for any repository change that aims to affect the final/end PDF output, including conversion scripts, LaTeX/frontmatter, typography, pagination, PDF metadata, or visual layout. Verification compiles source XML to LaTeX, reviews the generated LaTeX, compiles that LaTeX into PDF, then renders PDF pages as images for visual requirements review.

stages.title: Scope source XMLs and PDF requirements
stages.description: Confirm that this required PDF-affecting workflow applies, then identify the affected CME XML formats, representative source files, expected Pandoc outputs, and concrete PDF/LaTeX requirements before editing.

descriptor.purpose: Scope affected CME source XML formats and PDF/LaTeX requirements before implementation.
artifacts: stage report with source paths and requirements
acceptanceChecks: Affected XML formats and sample files are named
Required LaTeX/PDF visual behavior is explicit
No implementation files are edited during planning
isolationBoundary: Record uncertainty instead of inventing corpus behavior

promptInstructions: Use this custom graph whenever the requested change aims to affect the final/end PDF output. Inspect the repository before editing. Identify the affected XML format(s), conversion wrapper(s), source XML sample(s), generated output locations, and acceptance requirements.

For broad converter or PDF-layout changes, prefer the established representative set: Gawain and Everyman for dlpstextclass; CME301 and CME302 for ets-temphead-eebo; recent_changes/additions_2023 CME301 and CME302 for ets-header-eebo; JulianRev for tei2; and headwords_forms_and_IDs.202112.xml for headwords.
State which requirements must be verified in generated LaTeX and which must be verified visually from rendered PDF page images. Do not edit product files in this stage.

stages.title: Implement bounded converter or PDF-output change
stages.description: Make the smallest repository change needed for the requested CME XML/Pandoc/LaTeX/PDF behavior while keeping docs and generated-artifact policy synchronized.

descriptor.purpose: Implement the bounded General-Corpus converter or PDF-output change.
outputs: Focused local validation evidence when produced
acceptanceChecks: Implementation is limited to the requested converter/PDF behavior
Docs/config stay synchronized with behavior
Generated artifacts remain under ignored build paths
isolationBoundary: Do not commit or push from this stage

promptInstructions: Implement only the requested CME XML/Pandoc/LaTeX/PDF behavior. Keep generated files under build/ or another ignored location.
Update docs or helper scripts when behavior or invocation changes. Preserve existing command-line compatibility unless the user explicitly requested a breaking change.
Do not commit or push. Record changed files and any focused smoke checks run before the verification stages.

stages.title: Compile source XML to Pandoc LaTeX
stages.description: Generate Pandoc LaTeX from the affected source XML files and capture exact commands, logs, and generated .tex paths.

descriptor.purpose: Compile affected CME source XML files to Pandoc-generated LaTeX.
acceptanceChecks: Each scoped source XML produces a .tex file
Commands and logs are recorded
Converter failures are classified with actionable context
isolationBoundary: Do not edit product files in compile verification
Use Pandoc-generated LaTeX, not hand-written substitute TeX

promptInstructions: Compile each scoped source XML to a .tex file with scripts/pandoc-cme-xml or the appropriate scripts/pandoc-* wrapper. Store generated LaTeX and logs under build/task-graph/ or another ignored build directory.
For broad PDF-output changes, run the representative set named in the planning stage; for targeted changes, run the affected source plus at least one nearby representative sample if practical.
When frontmatter, metadata, titles, dates, or source provenance are affected, run scripts/audit-frontmatter-latex.py against the relevant scope or all CME/source XML files and record the report path.
Capture exact commands, exit codes, generated .tex paths, and any parser/recovery warnings. Fail this stage if Pandoc/HTML conversion does not produce usable LaTeX.

stages.title: Review generated LaTeX
stages.description: Inspect the generated LaTeX files directly before PDF compilation, checking frontmatter, metadata, text structure, escaping, and absence of unwanted source artifacts.

descriptor.purpose: Review generated Pandoc LaTeX before compiling it into PDF.
outputs: LaTeX review findings or PASS report
artifacts: LaTeX review report with file/line references when applicable
acceptanceChecks: Generated LaTeX is inspected directly
Frontmatter/order/metadata requirements are checked
Findings cite generated .tex paths and relevant lines
isolationBoundary: Do not edit files during review
Do not skip LaTeX review by relying only on PDF output

promptInstructions: Review the generated .tex files, not only source scripts. Check title page, colophon, table of contents order, document class, font variables/includes, source provenance placement, original/source dates, title/subtitle completeness, and body structure as applicable to the request.
Check for unwanted visible source markers, raw XML/HTML leakage, broken escaping, duplicated metadata, or prose/verse/list structures that are likely to compile poorly.
If the request affects a specific behavior such as pilcrow splitting, cite representative generated LaTeX snippets showing the behavior. Do not edit product files in this stage.

stages.title: Compile reviewed LaTeX into PDF
stages.description: Compile the reviewed Pandoc-generated LaTeX files into PDFs with XeLaTeX/latexmk and capture logs, page counts, and severe warnings.

descriptor.purpose: Compile reviewed Pandoc-generated LaTeX into PDF artifacts.
acceptanceChecks: PDFs are built from the reviewed .tex files
LaTeX failures are actionable
PDF page counts and paths are recorded
isolationBoundary: Do not edit product files in PDF compile verification
Do not bypass the reviewed .tex by generating unrelated PDFs

promptInstructions: Compile the reviewed .tex files into PDFs using latexmk -xelatex -interaction=nonstopmode -halt-on-error, or an equivalent XeLaTeX flow required by the repository. Use the reviewed .tex paths as inputs.
Store PDFs and logs under build/task-graph/ or another ignored build directory. Run pdfinfo or an equivalent command to record page counts and PDF metadata needed for review.
Treat LaTeX errors as failures. Surface severe overfull boxes, missing fonts, missing includes, missing images, or frontmatter build errors with file/log context.

stages.title: Render PDFs to images and visually review requirements
stages.description: Render representative PDF pages to images/contact sheets, view those images, and determine whether the visible PDF requirements are satisfied.

descriptor.purpose: Render PDF pages as images and visually verify PDF requirements.
acceptanceChecks: Representative PDF pages are rendered to images
Images are viewed or analyzed before pass/fail judgment
Visible requirements are explicitly marked pass/fail/unknown
isolationBoundary: Do not rely on text extraction alone for visual quality
Do not edit product files in the visual review stage

promptInstructions: Render representative PDF pages to images using pdftoppm, ImageMagick, or an equivalent local tool. Include title page, colophon, table of contents, first body page, and pages around the changed behavior; for long works, sample beginning/middle/end plus targeted pages.

Create a contact sheet when multiple pages are rendered. View the rendered images directly in Pi when useful, and use ui_feedback_analyze for non-secret local screenshots/assets when heuristic visual feedback would help.

Evaluate visible requirements such as book-style frontmatter order, typography/font suitability, headings, paragraph/verse breaks, overflow, blank pages, widows/orphans where relevant, and absence of visible source markers. Record image paths and pass/fail/unknown findings.

stages.title: Review evidence and requirements satisfaction
stages.description: Review the implementation diff and all LaTeX/PDF/visual evidence before handoff, ensuring requirements are satisfied or gaps are explicit.

descriptor.purpose: Review final diff and verification evidence for General-Corpus PDF work.
outputs: Final PASS/FAIL review with remaining risks
acceptanceChecks: All user requirements are mapped to evidence
Generated artifacts remain ignored
Remaining gaps are explicit before handoff
isolationBoundary: Do not modify implementation during final review

promptInstructions: Review the git diff plus recorded verification artifacts. Confirm the task included XML-to-LaTeX compilation, direct LaTeX review, LaTeX-to-PDF compilation, and visual PDF image review.
Map each user requirement to evidence paths and exact commands where possible. Check that build artifacts are ignored and source/config files intended for the repo are tracked.
Return PASS only if requirements are satisfied by evidence; otherwise list blocking gaps and the stage that must be rerun. Do not commit or push.
